relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The spiritual ones restore the overtaken person with gentleness, not superiority.
Bearing one another’s burdens fulfills Messiah’s Law.
Self-importance deceives the one who is nothing by oneself.
Each tests their own work without living by comparison.
Each also carries their own load.
The instructed one shares good things with the instructor.
Sowing and reaping cannot be mocked.
Sowing into flesh-pattern reaps decay; sowing into Spirit reaps age-long life.
Do not grow weary in doing good; harvest comes in season.
Work the good toward all, especially the household of trust.
Paul marks the letter with large letters by his own hand.
Those compelling circumcision want a good face in flesh and to avoid the cross’s offense.
They want to boast in another’s flesh rather than keep Law themselves.
Paul boasts only in the cross, where world and self are crucified to one another.
Neither circumcision nor uncircumcision is the center: new creation is.
Peace and mercy rest on those walking by this rule, and upon the Israel of the Divine.
Paul carries the marks of Jesus in his body.
Grace of Lord Jesus Messiah rests with their spirit.

contemplative_note: Galatians 6 completes freedom as embodied repair: gentle restoration, burden-bearing, sowing into Spirit, and new creation beyond every status-marker.
